chunk_map.heading: Orient and *map the window* (H1 + title block)
Ask what Claude skipped, and why
Read the self-report, then spot-check it
Check how full the window is

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 5 line 72: "the bug you brought from prework" — named verbatim in the compendium's own session-artifacts carve-out example list; exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 5 line 72: "The next exercise fixes the bug you brought from prework" — confirmed against this file's own maintainer Arc block ("Hands off to: fix-tests-first — the bug gets fixed in the window you just mapped"); the referenced beat exists.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Live-demo beats survive a text read.

rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 5 "Read the unread slice": lines 70-72: the header promises an action ("Read the unread slice") but the three bullets only discuss the significance of the unread percentage and hand off to the next exercise — no prompt or instruction has the student actually read the unread slice's contents.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Retitle the chunk around what it delivers (the window-map recap), or add the missing beat: a prompt that has the student look at what's in the unread portion.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Checked sub-items: 'Not X. Not Y. Z.' construction — none. Anaphora/epistrophe triples — none. Self-grading superlatives — none. Could be more, could be less") is the compendium's own named maintainer-attested exception (accept-with-note, no edit); this file's own maintainer block confirms the attestation. rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
